right, and I've got Rachel Baxter on my left.

I've got lots of support,
but I, I was on the board for 12 years then, then, um, left

It's an organization that I've, in terms
of predecessor organizations, uh, worked alongside
for many, many years now.
I've sort of been, um, part of the operation for many years
and it keeps on getting better.

and we have a, um, can someone Collect this?

Um, um, uh, which you can, you can study hard in a second.
Um, but, uh, in, in a rapidly changing global environment,
international trade has never been more important
to New Zealand's prosperity from food
and fiber to tech, manufacturing, tourism
and services exports are the cornerstone
of our economic growth.
With over $108 billion supporting one in four jobs,
uh, growing exports is a key pillar
of New Zealand's economic strategy
under the government's aspiration to double the value
of exports over 10 years.
NZT is supporting businesses to scale
and to grow internationally for the good of New Zealand
to help deliver a more prosperous,
productive New Zealand economy.
But it is really difficult out there.
We have lots of challenges, mainly, um, uh, tariffs
imposed by the United States
and the distortions they're imposing.
And we have a slowing China, so it's not easy
for our exporters.
But we've had a really good year
as NZTE achieving record results
alongside a reduced budget.
And this is why we're just giving you that chart,
which exemplifies that fact.
Uh, NZTE took up this challenge
and focused on increasing productivity
and having impact doing more for less.
NZT has helped businesses deliver deals
that generated a record 4.83 billion in
export value to New Zealand.
A 27% increase over the previous financial year
with a smaller operating budget.
We achieved a record 3,832
International export growth outcomes.
These are things such as new channels to market,
employing people, finding partners.
We supported the delivery
of 17 government led trade missions so far,
this parliamentary term, helping to open doors for hundreds
of business delegates
and delivering over 200 commercial outcomes valued at more
than $2 billion in projected trade value.
And we achieved a positive 78 net promoter score
from the export customers we work with,
demonstrating they find significant value in our support.
On the investment side, we supported 85 investment deals
that resulted in the potential direct economic impact
of 5.4 billion against the target of 5 billion, an increase
of 19% on the previous year.
And the realization of a longstanding internal goal
to achieve 5 billion, um, PDEI.
By 2025, we saw an 11% rise in the number
of investors while the value
of NZ T'S investment pipeline expanded by over 60 billion,
a 334% increase on the previous year.
And we supported the establishment of Invest New Zealand.
In fact, Peter and I were just up in Auckland hearing the
minister, um, give a speech
and announce the new board of, uh, invest,
uh, invest New Zealand.
Uh, so our work has almost been done in that regard.
But we, we supported the establishment of Invest New Zealand
as a new entity, as part of a joint project with mb
with the launch occurring on one July, 2025.
And while we received a reduction in funding
of 3.5 million this financial year from 179.3
to $175.8 million, we also increased
results in all areas
by focusing on core business lifting productivity.
We're working hard to get more for less
with investment in New Zealand now operating
as a separate entity.
And NZ t's return to a single mandate
of supporting the growth of New Zealand exporters.
We continue to deliver on our core strategy,
focusing our support on the thousand most ambitious growth
oriented companies where we have,
we we can have the most impact.
We're focusing with more intensity on emerging high growth
companies and rapidly growing larger high potential firms.
Looking ahead, NZTE is laser focused on supporting the
government's going for growth strategy, working closely
with our partner agencies, MB MPI, tourism education
and others, um, at co being another, uh,
to grow exports while delivering real value
to New Zealand's economy as part
of a crosscutting government approach.
There's no doubt global headwinds are creating challenging
economic conditions for our exporters, leading
to market uncertainty, rising tariffs
and supply chain issues.
Our latest customer survey also showed that scaling
and growing a business and accessing the funding
and resources to grow are impeding our exporters.
There are bright spots too.
The economy grew by 10, export economy grew by 10.

The export economy had grown by 10%.
And FFY 25 business confidence

Free trade agreements, um, which are negotiated

are opening up new, new markets.
Our exporters are finding new high value niches

2025, and tech and services continue to grow at pace.
Technologically, we're entering a new frontier
where investment in AI
and digital infrastructure will determine our
global competitiveness.
Helping companies navigate these challenges
and leverage these opportunities remain central
to our work for the good of New Zealand.
